back_translation: My brothers, do not let many of you become teachers, because you know that we who are teachers will receive a heavier judgment
For we all stumble in many matters. If anyone does not stumble in speech, that person is mature, able to control his whole body
When we put a bridle into a horse’s mouth so it will obey us, we can also control its whole body
Look at ships — though they are very large, and are driven along by strong winds, they are still steered by a small rudder, in whatever direction the helmsman wants
In the same way, the tongue is a small organ, but it boasts of great things. Look at how a small fire can set a great forest ablaze
And the tongue is a fire, a world of unrighteousness among our body parts. The tongue defiles the entire body, sets the cycle of life ablaze, and is itself set on fire by the fire of hell
For all kinds of wild animals and birds, reptiles and sea creatures, are being tamed and have been tamed by humans
But the tongue, no human can tame. It is a restless evil, full of deadly poison
With that tongue, we bless the Lord who is our Father, and with that same tongue we curse humans who were created according to the likeness of God
Out of the same mouth come both blessing and curse. My brothers, this should not be so
Does a spring not gush both fresh water and bitter water from the same opening?
My brothers, can a fig tree produce olives? Or a grapevine produce figs? Salt water cannot produce fresh water either
Is there anyone among you who has wisdom and understanding? Let that person show it by good conduct, by deeds done in the gentleness of wisdom
But if you have bitter jealousy and selfish ambition in your heart, do not boast and lie against the truth
Such wisdom does not come from above, but is wisdom that is earthly, natural, and demonic
For where there is jealousy and selfish ambition, there is disorder and every evil practice
But the wisdom that comes from above is, first, pure wisdom; then it is peaceable, gentle, easily-obeying, full of mercy and good fruits, impartial, and not hypocritical
And the fruit of righteousness is sown in peace, for those who make peace
